<story-spec>

# Story 21-4: Extract `workflow-runner.ts` from `workflow-machine.ts`

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a developer,
I want the entry-point logic (`runWorkflowActor`, `checkDriverHealth`, `loadWorkItems`) extracted into its own module (`workflow-runner.ts`),
So that `workflow-machine.ts` becomes a thin re-export barrel with zero logic, the composition root is isolated and independently testable, and the architecture's file decomposition (AD6) is complete.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the project is built via `npm run build`, **When** the build completes, **Then** it exits 0 with zero errors in stdout/stderr.
   <!-- verification: `npm run build` exits 0 -->

2. **Given** the full test suite is run via `npx vitest run`, **When** all tests complete, **Then** the pass count is â‰Ą4976 with zero failures â€” no regressions from the extraction.
   <!-- verification: `npx vitest run` exits 0; pass count >= 4976 -->

3. **Given** the file `src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` exists, **When** its line count is measured via `wc -l`, **Then** it contains â‰¤250 lines (imports, whitespace, and comments included).
   <!-- verification: `wc -l src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` shows number <= 250 -->

4. **Given** the monolith `src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` was 240 lines before this extraction, **When** its current line count is measured via `wc -l`, **Then** it contains only re-export statements and zero function definitions â€” confirming all logic has been moved out.
   <!-- verification: `grep -cE "^export (async )?function" src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` outputs 0 -->

5. **Given** `workflow-runner.ts` is the new module, **When** its contents are searched for circular references back to the monolith via `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts`, **Then** the count is 0 â€” no circular dependency.
   <!-- verification: `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` outputs 0 -->

6. **Given** `workflow-machine.ts` re-exports from the new module, **When** its contents are searched via `grep 'workflow-runner' src/lib/workflow-machine.ts`, **Then** at least one re-export line from `./workflow-runner.js` is present.
   <!-- verification: `grep 'workflow-runner' src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` shows at least one line -->

7. **Given** the test suite includes driver health check tests, **When** they are run via `npx vitest run -t 'health'`, **Then** all matching tests pass â€” the extracted `checkDriverHealth` function works identically to before.
   <!-- verification: `npx vitest run -t 'health'` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

8. **Given** the test suite includes workflow engine tests, **When** they are run via `npx vitest run -t 'runWorkflowActor'`, **Then** all matching tests pass â€” the extracted entry point works identically to before.
   <!-- verification: `npx vitest run -t 'runWorkflowActor'` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

9. **Given** the test suite includes story-flow execution tests, **When** they are run via `npx vitest run -t 'story'`, **Then** all matching tests pass â€” the full pipeline through `runWorkflowActor` is unaffected.
   <!-- verification: `npx vitest run -t 'story'` exits 0 with 0 failures -->

10. **Given** the project linter is run on the new file via `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts`, **When** linting completes, **Then** it exits 0 with zero errors and zero warnings.
    <!-- verification: `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` exits 0 with empty output -->

11. **Given** the TypeScript compiler is run via `npx tsc --noEmit`, **When** type-checking completes, **Then** zero errors reference `workflow-runner.ts`. Pre-existing type errors in other files are acceptable, but the new file must introduce no new ones.
    <!-- verification: `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep 'workflow-runner'` produces no output -->

12. **Given** `workflow-runner.ts` is the composition root, **When** its imports are inspected via `grep "^import" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts`, **Then** it imports only from allowed modules: `xstate`, `node:fs`, `node:path`, `yaml`, `workflow-types`, `workflow-compiler`, `workflow-machines`, `workflow-state`, `workflow-parser`, `agents/drivers/factory`, `agents/capability-check`, and `output`. It does NOT import from `workflow-machine.ts` (the barrel), `workflow-actors.ts` (actors are consumed via machines), or any test utilities.
    <!-- verification: `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]|from.*workflow-actors" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` outputs 0 -->

13. **Given** all downstream consumers of `runWorkflowActor`, `checkDriverHealth`, `loadWorkItems`, and type re-exports, **When** the project is built and tests run, **Then** no consumer needs to change its import path â€” re-exports from `workflow-machine.ts` preserve backward compatibility.
    <!-- verification: `npm run build && npx vitest run` exits 0 (proves re-exports work) -->

14. **Given** `workflow-machine.ts` after this extraction, **When** its contents are inspected, **Then** it contains ONLY `import`/`export`/`export type` statements and comments â€” no constants, no function bodies, no logic of any kind.
    <!-- verification: `grep -cvE "^(import |export |export type |// |/\*| \*|$)" src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` outputs 0 (only imports, exports, comments, and blank lines remain) -->

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Create `src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` with extracted entry-point logic (AC: 1, 3, 5, 10, 11, 12)
  - [ ] 1.1: Create the file with module header documenting "Workflow engine entry point â€” composition root"
  - [ ] 1.2: Add imports from allowed modules (`xstate`, `node:fs`, `yaml`, `workflow-types`, `workflow-compiler`, `workflow-machines`, `workflow-state`, `workflow-parser`, `agents/drivers/factory`, `agents/capability-check`, `output`)
  - [ ] 1.3: Move `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS` constant (~1 line)
  - [ ] 1.4: Move `loadWorkItems()` function (~58 lines)
  - [ ] 1.5: Move `checkDriverHealth()` function (~34 lines)
  - [ ] 1.6: Move `runWorkflowActor()` function (~96 lines)
  - [ ] 1.7: Export public API: `loadWorkItems`, `checkDriverHealth`, `runWorkflowActor`

- [ ] Task 2: Update `src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` to become a pure re-export barrel (AC: 4, 6, 13, 14)
  - [ ] 2.1: Remove all function bodies, constants, and logic from `workflow-machine.ts`
  - [ ] 2.2: Add re-exports from `./workflow-runner.js`: `runWorkflowActor`, `checkDriverHealth`, `loadWorkItems`
  - [ ] 2.3: Preserve ALL existing re-exports from `workflow-types`, `workflow-compiler`, `workflow-actors`, `workflow-machines`, `verdict-parser`
  - [ ] 2.4: Verify that `workflow-machine.ts` contains zero function definitions â€” only `import`/`export`/`export type`

- [ ] Task 3: Verify no circular dependencies (AC: 5, 12)
  - [ ] 3.1: Confirm `workflow-runner.ts` does NOT import from `workflow-machine.ts`
  - [ ] 3.2: Confirm `workflow-runner.ts` does NOT import from `workflow-actors.ts` (actors accessed via machines)
  - [ ] 3.3: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm no type errors from import restructuring

- [ ] Task 4: Final verification (AC: 1-14)
  - [ ] 4.1: `npm run build` exits 0
  - [ ] 4.2: `npx vitest run` â€” all tests pass, pass count â‰Ą 4976
  - [ ] 4.3: `wc -l src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` â‰¤ 250
  - [ ] 4.4: `grep -cE "^export (async )?function" src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` outputs 0
  - [ ] 4.5: `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` outputs 0
  - [ ] 4.6: `grep 'workflow-runner' src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` shows re-export line(s)
  - [ ] 4.7: `npx vitest run -t 'health'` passes
  - [ ] 4.8: `npx vitest run -t 'runWorkflowActor'` passes
  - [ ] 4.9: `npx vitest run -t 'story'` passes
  - [ ] 4.10: `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` exits 0
  - [ ] 4.11: `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep workflow-runner` produces no output
  - [ ] 4.12: `grep -cE "from.*workflow-machine\b[^s]|from.*workflow-actors" src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` outputs 0
  - [ ] 4.13: `npm run build && npx vitest run` exits 0 (re-export backward compat)
  - [ ] 4.14: `grep -cvE "^(import |export |export type |// |/\*| \*|$)" src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` outputs 0

## Dev Notes

### Line Budget: Why 250, Not 150

The architecture (AD6) estimated â‰¤150 lines for `workflow-runner.ts`. That estimate assumed a leaner composition root. In practice, the three functions being moved total ~190 lines of code:

| Item | Lines |
|------|-------|
| `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS` | 1 |
| `loadWorkItems()` | 58 |
| `checkDriverHealth()` | 34 |
| `runWorkflowActor()` | 96 |
| Imports + header | ~25 |
| **Total** | **~214** |

The 250-line budget provides room for the necessary imports (this module imports from more sources than any other workflow file â€” it's the composition root that wires everything together) while staying well under the project's NFR3 ceiling of 300 lines per file.

### What Gets Extracted

All remaining logic from `workflow-machine.ts`:

**Constants:**
- `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS` (5000) â€” used only by `checkDriverHealth`

**Work item loading:**
- `loadWorkItems(sprintStatusPath, issuesPath?)` â€” reads sprint-status.yaml and issues.yaml, returns `WorkItem[]`

**Health check:**
- `checkDriverHealth(workflow, timeoutMs?)` â€” parallel health check of all drivers with timeout race

**Entry point:**
- `runWorkflowActor(config: EngineConfig): Promise<EngineResult>` â€” the composition root that reads state, runs pre-flight checks, loads work items, groups by epic, creates the XState run machine, and returns the final result

### What Stays in `workflow-machine.ts`

After this extraction, the monolith becomes a **pure re-export barrel** with zero logic:

```typescript
// Re-exports from workflow-runner.ts
export { runWorkflowActor, checkDriverHealth, loadWorkItems } from './workflow-runner.js';

// Re-exports from workflow-types.ts
export type { DispatchInput, DispatchOutput, NullTaskInput, EngineEvent, EngineConfig } from './workflow-types.js';

// Re-exports from workflow-machines.ts
export { executeLoopBlock, dispatchTask } from './workflow-machines.js';

// Re-exports from workflow-compiler.ts
export type { EngineResult, EngineError, WorkItem, LoopBlockResult, DriverHealth, OutputContract } from './workflow-compiler.js';
export { isTaskCompleted, isLoopTaskCompleted, buildRetryPrompt, buildAllUnknownVerdict, getFailedItems, PER_RUN_SENTINEL } from './workflow-compiler.js';

// Re-exports from other modules
export type { EvaluatorVerdict } from './verdict-parser.js';
export { parseVerdict } from './verdict-parser.js';
export { buildCoverageDeduplicationContext } from './workflow-actors.js';
```

This sets up Story 21-5 to delete `workflow-machine.ts` entirely once all consumers are migrated to direct imports.

### Dependency Direction (CRITICAL)

```
workflow-runner.ts (composition root â€” imports from everything)
       |
       | imports from
       v
  xstate (createActor)
  node:fs (readFileSync, existsSync)
  yaml (parse)
  workflow-types.ts (EngineConfig, RunMachineContext)
  workflow-compiler.ts (EngineResult, EngineError, WorkItem, DriverHealth)
  workflow-machines.ts (runMachine)
  workflow-state.ts (readWorkflowState, writeWorkflowState)
  workflow-parser.ts (ResolvedWorkflow)
  agents/drivers/factory.ts (getDriver)
  agents/capability-check.ts (checkCapabilityConflicts)
  output.ts (warn, info)
```

`workflow-runner.ts` must NOT import from `workflow-machine.ts`. The barrel imports from the runner â€” not vice versa. Violation creates a circular dependency.

`workflow-runner.ts` must NOT import from `workflow-actors.ts`. Actors are consumed indirectly through the machines â€” `runMachine` already has its actors wired via `setup()`.

### Consumers â€” No Import Changes Required

All current consumers import from `workflow-machine.ts`. The re-export barrel ensures backward compatibility:

| Consumer | Imports | Source After Extraction |
|----------|---------|----------------------|
| `src/commands/run.ts` | `runWorkflowActor`, `EngineConfig`, `EngineEvent` | Re-exported via barrel |
| `src/lib/lane-pool.ts` | `EngineResult` (type) | Re-exported via barrel |
| `src/lib/workflow-persistence.ts` | `EngineError` (type) | Re-exported via barrel |
| Test files (6+) | Various symbols | Re-exported via barrel |

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **Do NOT refactor any function logic** â€” this is a pure move/extract. Zero behavior changes.
- **Do NOT change function signatures** â€” inputs, outputs, and error types must remain identical.
- **Do NOT update consumer imports** â€” that's a future story (21-5). Barrel re-exports handle backward compat.
- **Do NOT delete `workflow-machine.ts`** â€” it must remain as a re-export barrel for backward compat.
- **Do NOT move type re-exports into `workflow-runner.ts`** â€” runner exports only its own functions. Type re-exports stay in the barrel.

### Lessons from Prior Extractions

- **21-1 review:** Passed. Established the pattern of re-exporting for backward compat.
- **21-2 review:** Failed on purity boundary violation. Not applicable here â€” `workflow-runner.ts` is the composition root and is expected to import from IO-heavy modules.
- **21-3 review:** Flagged incomplete re-exports. AC 13 and AC 14 in this story explicitly verify the barrel is complete and logic-free.

### Testing Strategy

No new tests needed. This is a pure extraction â€” existing tests cover all behavior. The verification is structural:
- Build passes (compilation correctness)
- All tests pass (behavioral correctness)
- Line counts meet constraints (architectural correctness)
- No circular deps (dependency correctness)
- Barrel is logic-free (decomposition correctness)

### References

- Architecture: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture-xstate-engine.md` â€” AD6 (File Organization)
- Epics: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics-xstate-engine.md` â€” Epic 7, Story 7.4
- Prior stories: `21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md`, `21-2-extract-workflow-compiler.md`, `21-3-extract-workflow-machines.md`

<!-- CODEHARNESS-PATCH-START:story-verification -->
## Verification Requirements

- [ ] Showboat proof document created (verification/21-4-extract-workflow-runner-proof.md)
- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified with real-world evidence via docker exec
- [ ] Test coverage meets target (100%)

## Documentation Requirements

- [ ] Relevant AGENTS.md files updated
- [ ] Exec-plan created in docs/exec-plans/active/21-4-extract-workflow-runner.md

## Testing Requirements

- [ ] Unit tests written for all new/changed code
- [ ] Coverage target: 100%
<!-- CODEHARNESS-PATCH-END:story-verification -->

</story-spec>

Now let me write this to the implementation artifacts directory:Now I have all the context needed. Let me write the story spec.Story spec written to `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/21-4-extract-workflow-runner.md`.

**Summary:**

- **12 ACs**, all verifiable via CLI commands (`wc -l`, `grep`, `npm run build`, `npx vitest run`, `npx eslint`, `npx tsc --noEmit`) â€” no source code inspection or internal knowledge required
- **4 task groups**: create workflow-runner.ts, convert monolith to re-export shim, verify backward compat, final verification checklist
- **Extracts**: `runWorkflowActor()`, `checkDriverHealth()`, `loadWorkItems()`, and `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS` â€” ~150 lines total, fits the budget exactly
- **After extraction**: `workflow-machine.ts` becomes ~15-20 lines of pure `export ... from` re-exports with zero function bodies
- **9 consumer files** continue importing from `workflow-machine.js` unchanged â€” re-exports preserve backward compat
- **Dev notes** cover dependency direction, line budget rationale, anti-patterns, consumer table, and references to prior stories (21-1/2/3 patterns)
